title: EP-ADMISSIBILITY-PROFILE-v1
description: A NAMED, content-addressed admissibility bar a relying party can cite by id ('for agentic payments above X, require ep:admissibility:money-movement:v1') instead of hand-assembling freshness/assurance/requirement knobs. A profile is authored and PINNED by the relying party; the verdict is computed OFFLINE and deterministically against the pinned profile_hash. EMILIA neither hosts an authoritative registry nor adjudicates and is not in the trust path: it publishes this interoperable SCHEMA and clearly-labeled REFERENCE profiles a relying party may fork, on identical terms to everyone. Field names and types mirror lib/evidence/admissibility-profiles.js (defineAdmissibilityProfile); the closed verdict precedence and requirement grammar live in lib/evidence/admissibility.js. See docs/EP-ADMISSIBILITY-PROFILE-SPEC.md and docs/EP-ADMISSIBILITY-SPEC.md.

id.description: Stable id a relying party cites, e.g. 'ep:admissibility:money-movement:v1'. Reference examples use the ep:admissibility: namespace; a relying party authoring its own SHOULD use its own namespace (e.g. 'acme:admissibility:wire:v3'). The id is a name, not an authority claim.

version.description: Profile content version, an integer. Bump when the bar changes so a pinned profile_hash and a cited version move together (defineAdmissibilityProfile defaults it to 1).

authored_by.description: WHO authored this bar. For reference examples this is EMILIA-as-example-author; for a real deployment it is the relying party that owns and pins the profile. Advisory provenance only, never a trust root: acceptance is by pinned profile_hash, not by this field. EMILIA is never the author of a relying party's bar.

requires.description: The list of evidence requirements. Non-optional entries gate (their absence yields missing_evidence); optional:true entries strengthen but do not gate. The evaluator derives the conjunction of all mandatory requires[].evidence types as the requirement and hands it to lib/evidence/admissibility.js under the closed precedence.

verdicts.description: The CLOSED verdict set this profile is evaluated under, echoing lib/evidence/admissibility.js (ADMISSIBILITY_VERDICTS). Stamped by defineAdmissibilityProfile and part of the hashed object. The precedence (unverifiable > conflicted > stale > missing_evidence > admissible) is fixed by the evaluator, not the profile. A default is always the weakest outcome, never admissible.

profile_hash.description: sha256: over the RFC-8785/JCS canonical bytes of this profile object with the profile_hash field itself excluded, exactly as EP hashes elsewhere (lib/canonical-json.js canonicalize + SHA-256; computeProfileHash in lib/evidence/admissibility-profiles.js). Only id, version, authored_by, requires, and verdicts are part of the hashed object; human-facing annotations (reliance_purpose, description, action_family) are carried OUTSIDE the profile so they never perturb the hash. The relying party PINS this value; a resolved profile whose recomputed hash does not equal the pinned value yields a non-admissible verdict or a refusal (fail closed). The name is convenience; the pinned hash is the commitment.

evidence.description: The component TYPE this requirement is about, a non-empty token matched against a bundle item's `evidence`/`type` field. Not a closed enum: relying parties may introduce their own component types. Common values: authorization_receipt, quorum_receipt, witness_quorum, delegation, policy_permit, workload_identity, execution_attestation, consumption_proof, transparency, recourse_reference.

min_assurance.description: Minimum assurance class, ordered self_asserted < basic < verified < high < very_high (ASSURANCE_ORDER in lib/evidence/admissibility-profiles.js). A leg below the floor, or naming an unknown class, does not count toward satisfaction (fail closed). Absent => no assurance floor on this leg.

max_staleness_sec.description: Maximum accepted age of this leg in seconds, relative to the evaluation time. An older-but-valid leg is 'stale', never accepted (the evidence exists but is not live). Absent => no freshness bound on this leg.

checks.description: Additional live/state checks this leg MUST pass. 'revocation_checked' is special: the leg's live revoked state MUST be false AND known (an unknown revocation state fails closed). Any other named check is looked up in the leg's own `checks` map and MUST be true; an unrecognized/absent check fails closed. Items are free strings so relying parties may define their own checks.

optional.description: When true, this requirement STRENGTHENS the bar (credited and evaluated when present) but its absence alone is not missing_evidence. A present-but-invalid optional leg still enters the fact set and is caught by the unverifiable/conflicted precedence. Optional legs never relax fail-closed behavior for the required legs.

params.description: Requirement-specific parameters hashed into the profile but not interpreted by the profile layer, e.g. { "k": 2 } for a witness_quorum threshold. Interpreted by the leg's own type verifier. Kept minimal and canonicalizable (no floats).
